zh.Broxmouth Roxburghshire: 英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安布洛姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當David Gray於1278年出生在蘇格蘭洛包爾時，他的父親Andrew Gray Lord of Broxmouth25歲，母親Ada Gifford21歲。他於1302年與Lady Christian Graves在蘇格蘭Forfarshire登迪結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1348年在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: McGreevy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Seamus, Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Riabhaigh, a patronymic from the personal name Riabhach ‘brindled, grizzled’. Compare Reavey and Creevy 2.

variants.name: Grayer
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English grei ‘gray’ (see Gray ) + her ‘hair’.

variants.name: Grays
meanings: English (Durham): variant of Gray , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or of Grace .

variants.name: Grice
meanings: English (Midlands): from Middle English grice, grise ‘pig’ (Old Norse gríss), probably a nickname or a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd.
English: nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle English grice, gris ‘gray’ (Old French gris, apparently of ancient Germanic origin, and probably a distant cognate of Gray 1).
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Greis  .

variants.name: Grey
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When David Gray was born in 1278, in Roxburghshire, Scotland, his father, Andrew Gray Lord of Broxmouth, was 25 and his mother, Ada Gifford, was 21. He married Lady Christian Graves in 1302, in Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1348, in Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

ko.lifeSummary: David Gray(이)가 1278년 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Andrew Gray Lord of Broxmouth(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada Gifford(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1302년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire 던디에서 Lady Christian Graves(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1348년 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
